ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTIN FOR AN ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO PERMIT DISCOVERY WITHIN THIRTY DAYS OF TRIAL SUBPOENA (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS VITALIY NEVIDOMY AND VOLHA SUSHKO'S MOTION TO QUASH DEFENDANTS DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE SUBPOENA IS OVERBROAD AND SEEKS INFORMATION THAT IS NOT EVEN CLAIMED TO BE RELEVANT AND NECESSARY TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT CHOSE NOT TO NARROW THE SCOPE OF SUBPOENA DURING MEET AND CONFER PROCESS TO (1) INQUIRE REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S PRESENCE AT WORK ON THE DATES OF ALLEGED HARASSMENT INCIDENTS AND (2) INQUIRE REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S WORK SCHEDULE/WORK HOURS DURING THE TIME IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING AND FOLLOWING THE TERMINATION OF TENANCY. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS REQUESTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Notice Of Motion For An Order Granting Leave To Permit Discovery Within 30 Days Of Trial
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel W. Wayne (415-649-6203) for Plaintiff/Moving party VITALIY NEVIDOMY and VOLHA SUSHKO.
Edward J. Rodzewich (510-457-3460) for Defendant/Moving party Marat Diner.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. The motion is hear concurrently with th motion for leave to permit discovery. Hearing is held. Argued re: Deposition, large amount of economic damages, narrowed only 2 years, Declaration of Richard Devine, diligence, and mistake in serving. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs MARAT DINER, KIRA DINER, and IGOR TYRYSHKIN's Motion For An Order Granting Leave To Permit Discovery Within 30 Days Of Trial is DENIED. Moving party failed to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 2024.050. Request for sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel W. Wayne (415-649-6203) for Plaintiff/Moving party VITALIY NEVIDOMY and VOLHA SUSHKO.
Edward J. Rodzewich (510-457-3460) for Defendant/Moving party Marat Diner.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. The motion is hear concurrently with th motion for leave to permit discovery. Hearing is held. Argued re: Deposition, large amount of economic damages, narrowed only 2 years, Declaration of Richard Devine, diligence, and mistake in serving. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs VITALIY NEVIDOMY and VOLHA SUSHKO's Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. The subpoena is overbroad and seeks information that is not even claimed to be relevant and necessary to defendant. Defendant chose not to narrow the scope of subpoena during meet and confer process to (1) inquire regarding Plaintiff's presence at work on the dates of alleged harassment incidents and (2) inquire regarding Plaintiff's work schedule/work hours during the time immediately preceding and following the termination of tenancy. Request for sanctions is granted as requested. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
